_note: DESIGN ARTIFACT (Phase 1). A profile is a named module set. The composer picks ONE base profile from the project profile (bootstrap/profile-project) then may add/drop modules based on detected facts. Every module listed here must exist in modules.json (validate-manifests enforces this).

generic.description: Stack-agnostic baseline. Used when the profiler can't confidently classify the stack.

python-fastapi.description: Python service: FastAPI/Pydantic, pytest, ruff, mypy, Postgres.

next-ts.description: TypeScript front end: Next.js, eslint, vitest, tsc.

python-next-fullstack.description: Python/FastAPI core + Next.js UI + Postgres, spec-driven with context bundles and the autonomy ladder.

security.description: Security-heavy posture on top of the generic baseline.

research-tooling.description: Research / experimentation workspace: notebooks, scratch tools, mixed small apps. Generic baseline plus context bundles and orchestration; stack modules (python/typescript/eval/loops) arrive via moduleSelectionRules from detected facts.

moduleSelectionRules._note: Hints the composer applies on top of the base profile, keyed off profile-project.json facts. Documented in docs/BOOTSTRAP.md. Rules on facts.* read MATERIALIZED facts (code on disk); rules on facts.intended.* read the spec-aware HINT inferred from doc/spec prose, so a spec-first project whose code does not yet exist still gets nudged toward the right modules. intended-keyed rules are a weaker signal than materialized ones; the composer should report when it adds a module on a spec hint alone.
